title: Christia convallaria
taxonomy: Christia convallaria is a species of plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to the Himalayas and parts of Asia. The plant is also known as the 'bengal orchid tree' due to its appearance and habit of growing in tropical forests. Christia convallaria is classified under the genus Christia and is closely related to the genus Coussapia. Christia convallaria is also known to be a member of the Rubiaceae family, which includes over 600 genera of flowering plants.
uses: Christia convallaria, also known as the Siraitia plant, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties.
